title: Meaty Red Pepper Brazilian Pork Grill
description: A salty and juicy Brazilian dish featuring the flavors of red pepper and tandoor spices, made plant-based-friendly and grilled on a cedar plank.

ingredients.name: cedar plank
ingredients.notes: soaked in water for at least 30 minutes

instructions: Soak the cedar plank in water for at least 30 minutes before grilling.
In a small bowl, mix together tandoor spice blend, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork tenderloin.
Heat the olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the sliced red bell pepper and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the Brazilian-style barbecue sauce to the skillet and stir to combine.
Place the pork tenderloin on the preheated grill, and brush with the barbecue sauce mixture.
Grill for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the pork reaches an internal temperature of 14525F.
Transfer the pork to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes.
To assemble the dish, place a slice of the grilled pork on a plate, top with a slice of red bell pepper, and garnish with chopped cilantro.
